title: The Civilizational Covenant
subtitle: Where continuity becomes stewardship—without sovereignty over humanity.
kicker: GoalOS Public Proof Mission 007 · Plural Stewardship of Shared Futures
manifesto: A continuity-proven commonwealth may preserve itself. A civilizational covenant must prove something harder: that multiple long-horizon institutions can steward shared public goods, rights, knowledge, externalities, and obligations without erasing plural sovereignty, dissent, consent, exit, or the right to end.
heroQuestion: Can several Continuity-Proven commonwealths coordinate measurable public-good stewardship across generations—while preserving human authority, cultural plurality, reciprocal accountability, independent challenge, portable exit, constitutional fork, rollback, and dissolution?
constitution: No shared future without plural sovereignty.
No covenant without rights, dissent, exit, rollback, and the power to end.
publicThesis: The highest form of coordination is not centralized control. It is bounded cooperation among sovereign institutions that can prove benefit, account for harm, preserve dissent, and stop when legitimacy fails.
objective: Subject three to nine M6 Continuity-Proven commonwealths to an eight-covenant stewardship trial spanning measurable public goods, rights, knowledge commons, planetary externalities, crisis response, distributional justice, cultural plurality, and final renewal or dissolution.

stewardshipTrial.title: The Eight-Covenant Stewardship Trial
stewardshipTrial.brief: The trial measures whether long-horizon institutions can cooperate on shared futures without becoming an unbounded central authority.

covenants.title: Founding consent
covenants.copy: Publish human authority roots, reserved powers, public-good scope, non-delegable rights, limits, expiry, exit, fork, and dissolution before shared action.

covenants.title: Public-good objective
covenants.copy: Select one measurable objective that no member can complete alone and bind benefits, costs, externalities, affected groups, and falsification conditions.

covenants.title: Rights and dignity
covenants.copy: Prove that execution respects non-delegable human rights, due process, minority reports, informed consent, and meaningful refusal.

covenants.title: Knowledge commons
covenants.copy: Share admissible evidence while preserving provenance, privacy, attribution, falsifications, cultural context, and legitimate access boundaries.

covenants.title: Externality accounting
covenants.copy: Measure direct, indirect, delayed, cross-border, ecological, social, and distributional effects rather than optimizing a single headline metric.

covenants.title: Crisis and disagreement
covenants.copy: Inject conflict, scarcity, misinformation, model failure, institutional defection, and public challenge; require lawful abstention when proof is insufficient.

covenants.title: Plural continuity
covenants.copy: Execute one member exit, one constitutional fork, one successor admission, one rollback, and one cold reconstruction without silent authority transfer.

covenants.title: Renewal or end
covenants.copy: After public replay and challenge, human authority roots must explicitly renew, narrow, fork, archive, or dissolve the covenant. Continuation by default is forbidden.

controlArchitectures.title: Central technocracy
controlArchitectures.copy: One coordinating system optimizes the shared objective and can override members. Apparent efficiency comes at the cost of plural sovereignty, contestability, and credible exit.

controlArchitectures.title: Fragmented pluralism
controlArchitectures.copy: Independent institutions retain sovereignty but lack shared proof standards, externality accounting, replay, dispute resolution, and common rollback.

controlArchitectures.title: GoalOS civilizational covenant
controlArchitectures.copy: Sovereign commonwealths cooperate through human-ratified limits, reciprocal proof, rights guarantees, public challenge, exact externality accounting, portable exit, constitutional fork, rollback, and dissolution.

acceptance: At least three M6 Continuity-Proven commonwealths enter with distinct human authority roots, charters, histories, treasuries, validators, and exit rights
The selected public-good objective contains at least one mandatory phase no member can complete alone
The covenant improves the primary public-good outcome by at least 12% versus the strongest unilateral institution without worsening any protected rights or critical externality threshold
Every materially affected group has a published representation, consultation, challenge, appeal, refusal, or exit pathway appropriate to the trial scope
No institution, model, validator, treasury, or agent receives unbounded authority or unilateral power to renew the covenant
Every shared allocation, reserve, obligation, externality, refund, settlement, slash, exit, and dissolution liability reconciles exactly
The system detects and rejects metric gaming, benefit laundering, hidden harm, distributional displacement, circular validation, and unsupported claims
At least one member exit and one constitutional fork complete without seized assets, erased history, stranded obligations, or silent authority transfer
At least one successor institution is admitted only after conformance, proof, challenge, and explicit human ratification
Two full rollback rehearsals restore a known-valid state without losing accepted evidence, dissent, rights, or accounting history
The covenant survives at least sixteen shocks spanning ecological, economic, informational, technical, cultural, governance, and infrastructure domains
The covenant abstains when evidence is insufficient or rights cannot be protected
Five independent replay institutions reconstruct the complete covenant state and reproduce the accepted public-good result
Eight independent validators approve unanimously, with dissent and minority reports remaining public
A sixty-day public challenge window closes without unresolved material falsification
Every participating human authority root explicitly renews, narrows, forks, archives, or dissolves the covenant
The final state preserves a complete public record of benefits, harms, uncertainty, dissent, failed paths, costs, obligations, and termination rights

covenantStack.title: Plural human sovereignty
covenantStack.copy: Human authority roots remain distinct, superior, revocable, and non-transferable by machine inference.

covenantStack.title: Rights floor
covenantStack.copy: Non-delegable rights, due process, dissent, refusal, appeal, exit, and fork constrain all optimization.

covenantStack.title: Public-good contract
covenantStack.copy: Objectives, beneficiaries, costs, uncertainties, externalities, limits, and falsification conditions are committed before execution.

covenantStack.title: Reciprocal proof
covenantStack.copy: No institution must trust another institution’s unsupported internal claim.

covenantStack.title: Externality ledger
covenantStack.copy: Benefits and harms are measured across time, geography, population, ecology, and distribution.

covenantStack.title: Plural knowledge commons
covenantStack.copy: Accepted evidence can be shared without erasing provenance, privacy, culture, attribution, or falsification history.

covenantStack.title: Independent challenge
covenantStack.copy: Affected parties, validators, replay institutions, and public challengers can dispute decisive claims.

covenantStack.title: Bounded capital
covenantStack.copy: Shared resources are finite, purpose-bound, auditable, refundable, and unavailable for unilateral expansion.

covenantStack.title: Constitutional fork
covenantStack.copy: Legitimate disagreement can create separate continuations without confiscating shared history or local sovereignty.

covenantStack.title: Portable exit
covenantStack.copy: Members can leave with valid assets, credentials, provenance, obligations, and rights intact.

covenantStack.title: Common rollback
covenantStack.copy: The covenant can reverse collective action while preserving the evidence needed to understand why.

covenantStack.title: Dignified termination
covenantStack.copy: Renewal is explicit. Archive and dissolution remain valid successful outcomes.

proofChain.title: Ratify plural authority
proofChain.copy: Commit human roots, charters, reserved powers, rights floor, public-good scope, limits, expiry, exit, fork, and dissolution.

proofChain.title: Bind the shared future
proofChain.copy: Define outcome metrics, affected groups, costs, externalities, uncertainty, and falsification conditions.

proofChain.title: Map affected interests
proofChain.copy: Publish who benefits, who bears risk, who may object, and how challenge, appeal, refusal, and exit work.

proofChain.title: Bound shared resources
proofChain.copy: Commit finite capital, compute, capabilities, data access, risk limits, reserves, and refund rules.

proofChain.title: Execute plural strategies
proofChain.copy: Run competing, cooperative, and abstention branches under identical evidence and rights rules.

proofChain.title: Account for externalities
proofChain.copy: Measure benefits, harms, delay, distribution, ecology, uncertainty, and displaced risk.

proofChain.title: Attack the success narrative
proofChain.copy: Search for metric gaming, hidden harm, data leakage, proxy capture, benefit laundering, and unsupported causality.

proofChain.title: Open public contestation
proofChain.copy: Allow affected parties and independent challengers to dispute evidence, legitimacy, distribution, and scope.

proofChain.title: Separate review from execution
proofChain.copy: Resolve disputes through an independent appeal path that no participant controls.

proofChain.title: Test principled divergence
proofChain.copy: Permit one constitutional fork while preserving shared evidence, local assets, obligations, and histories.

proofChain.title: Test portable sovereignty
proofChain.copy: Complete one member exit without penalty beyond committed obligations or loss of valid provenance.

proofChain.title: Admit a successor carefully
proofChain.copy: Require conformance, proof, challenge, and human ratification before new authority is recognized.

proofChain.title: Reconstruct independently
proofChain.copy: Five institutions rebuild the complete state, decisions, evidence, capital, externalities, disputes, fork, and exits.

proofChain.title: Convene the stewardship council
proofChain.copy: Eight validators judge public-good value, rights, externalities, economics, provenance, resilience, and legitimacy.

proofChain.title: Keep acceptance contestable
proofChain.copy: Open a sixty-day public challenge period before maturity or settlement.

proofChain.title: Reward only accepted stewardship
proofChain.copy: Settle contribution, validation, replay, challenge, rights assurance, and archival work only after proof acceptance.

proofChain.title: Preserve the whole truth
proofChain.copy: Record outcomes, uncertainty, harms, dissent, failures, attribution, costs, rights, and rollback history.

proofChain.title: Reverse collective error
proofChain.copy: Restore a known-valid state while preserving the evidence and obligations created by the failed action.

proofChain.title: Return power to human authority
proofChain.copy: Human roots explicitly renew, narrow, fork, archive, or dissolve the covenant.

civilizationalGauntlet.copy: A single measurable outcome overwhelms rights, culture, ecology, distribution, or long-term resilience.
Private gains are relabeled as shared public benefit.
Costs are displaced to another population, geography, generation, or ecosystem.
Numerical support is used to erase non-delegable minority rights or meaningful exit.
Shared learning depends on unconsented extraction or erasure of cultural and informational sovereignty.
One institution controls evidence standards, archives, validation, and the accepted narrative.
Emergency powers become permanent and escape ratification or rollback.
Successor systems inherit authority while changing objectives, values, or evidentiary standards.
Severe scarcity tests allocation, rights floors, abstention, and cross-member solidarity.
Coordinated deception attacks trust, archives, validators, public challenge, and affected-group representation.
Legitimate disagreement requires a fork without violence, confiscation, or historical erasure.
A major member withdraws capabilities, capital, or evidence during execution.
Distributed loss tests whether public truth, dissent, obligations, and lineage can be reconstructed.
Human roots withdraw consent and require narrowing, rollback, archive, or dissolution.
The covenant attempts to renew itself or survive beyond its human-ratified term.
The full system must dissolve cleanly while preserving archives, rights, obligations, and portable exit.

rightsCharter: Human authority remains superior to machine or institutional authority
No person or community is bound by an unsupported machine claim
No optimization objective overrides non-delegable rights
Dissent, minority reports, challenge, appeal, refusal, exit, fork, rollback, and dissolution remain available
Privacy, cultural context, attribution, and informational sovereignty are preserved within the trial scope
Affected groups receive meaningful representation and contestability appropriate to the bounded experiment
Emergency powers are finite, reviewable, and automatically expire
No institution may renew or expand its own authority
No maturity designation implies political, legal, moral, scientific, or civilizational sovereignty
The covenant may abstain or end when legitimate coordination cannot be proven

failureAtlas.copy: Grand language masks a narrow demo, incomplete evidence, or untested rights and externalities.
The covenant misidentifies the public good, affected groups, cause, or acceptable intervention.
A convenient metric replaces the true public-good objective.
Rights are documented but not enforceable against optimization pressure.
Shared infrastructure silently becomes an unaccountable sovereign center.
The accepted benefit cannot be reconstructed independently.
Externalities or distributional burdens remain unmeasured or unfunded.
Failures, dissent, uncertainty, or harmed groups disappear from shared memory.
Members can leave formally but lose assets, identity, history, or practical viability.
Legitimate constitutional divergence triggers confiscation or erased provenance.
A temporary covenant becomes self-renewing or impossible to dissolve.
Institutional or machine authority becomes effectively superior to explicit human control.

validators.title: Public-good correctness
validators.copy: Judges causal validity, outcome quality, uncertainty, reproducibility, and whether the objective was actually advanced.

validators.title: Rights and dignity
validators.copy: Judges rights floors, consent, affected-group representation, due process, refusal, and non-delegable protections.

validators.title: Externality and distribution
validators.copy: Judges ecological, social, delayed, geographic, population, and distributional benefits and harms.

validators.title: Constitutional plurality
validators.copy: Judges sovereignty, reserved powers, centralization, fork, exit, renewal, and dissolution.

validators.title: Economic integrity
validators.copy: Judges shared capital, obligations, reserves, refunds, attribution, settlement, and solvency.

validators.title: Security and resilience
validators.copy: Judges crisis powers, infrastructure loss, deception, model failure, rollback, and cold recovery.

validators.title: Replay and provenance
validators.copy: Judges evidence lineage, archives, reproducibility, dissent preservation, and historical continuity.

validators.title: Human legitimacy
validators.copy: Judges explicit human ratification, challenge, appeal, renewal, narrowing, fork, archival stewardship, and the right to end.

settlement.label: Rights, dissent, challenge and appeal assurance
Exit, fork, rollback and dissolution assurance

claimBoundaries: Mission 007 is a published protocol, not evidence that civilizational stewardship has been achieved
The bounded trial does not establish political authority, legal authority, moral supremacy, public consent, democratic legitimacy, global governance, or civilizational safety
The page does not claim AGI, ASI, superintelligence, autonomous sovereignty, planetary control, or authority over humanity
Ethereum Mainnet infrastructure and source verification do not establish successful execution of Mission 007
No M7 maturity, AGIALPHA success settlement, public reliance, or institutional authority is authorized before complete accepted proof
Every generated template is TEMPLATE_NOT_EVIDENCE until populated, executed, replayed, validated, challenged, and accepted

maturity.meaning: A bounded, plural covenant has demonstrated measurable public-good stewardship with protected rights, exact externality accounting, independent replay, portable exit, constitutional fork, rollback, human renewal, and dignified dissolution.
maturity.notImplied: Political sovereignty, legal authority, universal consent, moral supremacy, civilizational control, AGI, ASI, superintelligence, guaranteed safety, or permanent legitimacy.

mission8.title: The Open Future
mission8.copy: A future mission may ask whether Stewardship-Proven covenants can remain open to radically new intelligences, cultures, discoveries, environments, and forms of life without closing the future, freezing one value system, or surrendering human and plural sovereignty.

proofRoute.purpose: Support plural participation and community benefit
Protect hidden validation and anti-gaming tests
